interventions.text: Before the workday ends, write down each moment of panic during one high-pressure task and what set it off.
Each workday this week, log what triggers exam, interview, and presentation nerves.
Over the next 30 days at work, follow a short calm-down routine before every test, presentation, or meeting.
Over the next quarter at work, build steady calm under pressure through weekly timed practice runs.
Build a daily workday habit — a two-minute breathing reset before each high-pressure moment.
Practise this skill each week at work — rehearsing exams and presentations out loud until the nerves settle.
